title: Brazilian Paella with Red Potatoes
description: A twist on the classic Spanish dish, this Brazilian paella recipe adds red potatoes for a delicious and hearty meal.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add 1 onion, diced, and cook until translucent.
Add 2 cloves of garlic, minced, and cook for 1 minute.
Add 1 cup of uncooked rice and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add 2 cups of chicken broth and 1 cup of water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add 2 medium red potatoes, peeled and cubed, and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of Brazilian chili flake and 1 tablespoon of saffron threads.
Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes.
Garnish with spherified tapioca pearls and serve.
